Article Title: How to link Aadhaar card with UAN?
Article Text:
|

|

|

Here is the process of linking your Aadhaar with

account:

Online process of linking Aadhaar with EPF

|

|

Offline process of linking Aadhaar with EPF

While going to nearby field offices of EPFO or CSC outlets, keep these documents handy:

What is UAN? Universal Account Number (UAN) is a 12-digit number provided to each member of the Employees' Provided Fund Organisation (EPFO) through which he/she can manage his/her PF accounts. Is it mandatory to link Aadhaar to UAN?

No, it's not mandatory to link UAN to Aadhaar, however, doing so can make life simpler and the claims settlement easier. How to link Aadhaar to UAN?

Visit the EPFO website and login. Click on KYC update and enter your Aadhaar number. After verification, your UAN will be linked to Aadhaar. How to update UAN KYC?

UAN KYC can be updated via official website of the Employees' Provided Fund Organisation (EPFO).

The Employee Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) has made it mandatory to link Aadhaar number to Employee Provident Fund account. The EPFO has recently introduced most of its facilities online. Now you can check your PF account balance, statements, submit PF claims or partial withdrawal of money from your PF account. But to avail all these facilities, you need to link your Aadhaar with UAN and EPF account .Aadhaar is an important document for KYC verification as it contains details such as name, date of birth, address, photo etc. Nowadays, EPFO is providing all the benefits to the deserving beneficiaries directly into their respective bank account. So, linking your Aadhaar with UAN will merge all your PF accounts from different employers into one.1) Open the EPFO website - http://www.epfindia.com/site_en/2) Login using your UAN number and Password. To login, your UAN must be activated.3) Once you login, look at the top of the page to find Manage tab. Select the KYC option under Manage tab from the drop-down menu.4) Once you select KYC tab, you will find an online form having options to update Bank details, PAN details, Aadhaar, Passport, Driving License, Election Card, Ration Card or NPR.5) You can update all the documents here or if you want to update only Aadhaar then select Aadhaar, enter the Aadhaar number and also the name as it appears in Aadhaar Card. Then click on Save. You Aadhaar is now linked with UAN.Considering the fact that many employees and pensioners were facing problem into linking their Aadhaar to EPF online, The EPFO decided to launch the facility to update or link Aadhaar through offline mode. It introduced the facility named "Aadhaar Seeding Application" with support of the Common Service Centers (CSC) and CDAC. With the launch of Aadhaar Seeding Application facility, you can now go to the nearby field offices of EPFO or CSC outlets with some ready documents like UAN and Aadhaar to link your Aadhaar with the UAN.* Universal Account Number (UAN)* Copy of a PAN Card* Copy of your Aadhaar Card* IFSC Code of given bank* Account Number* One Cancel Check
Article Summary: Universal Account Number (UAN) is a 12-digit number provided to each member of the Employees' Provided Fund Organisation (EPFO) through which he/she can manage his/her PF accounts. It's not mandatory to link UAN to Aadhaar, however, doing so can make life simpler and the claims settlement easier.

Article Title: Aadhaar Driving Licence Link: How to link Aadhaar to Driving Licence
Article Text:
Here is a step-by-step guide to help you:

Is it mandatory to link Aadhaar to driving licence?

No, it's not mandatory to link Aadhaar to driving licence, however, doing so may make user verification an easier process. Can you link Aadhaar to Driving Licence online?

Yes, linking of Aadhaar to driving licence can be done online via official website of the state transport. What are the documents needed for Aadhaar-Driving Licence linking?

You just need Aadhaar linked mobile number for linking to Aadhaar number to driving licence. How to link Aadhaar to Driving Licence?

Visit official website of state transport, select Aadhaar Number Entry and follow the verification process as asked on the page to link Aadhaar to driving licence.

Just like other important documents and accounts, linking of Driving Licence with Aadhaar is not mandatory but important. As Driving Licence is issued by state road transport department, the process may vary from state to state, however broadly it remains the same.1) Visit the official website of your state's road transport department2) Select the 'Aadhaar Number Entry' on the page. Next, select the 'Search Element' as Registration number or License. Then, enter your Registration or License number and click on 'Get Details' icon. Once you click, your vehicle details will be shown3) Now enter Aadhaar number and mobile number in the column provided4) Click on submit. Once you click on Submit, an OTP will be sent to your registered mobile number, Enter that OTP and click to confirm.Once the details entered by you is confirmed by the transport officials, you will receive a notification on your mobile number and email ID.
Article Summary: The process may vary from state to state, however broadly it remains the same. Visit official website of your state's road transport department. Select the 'Aadhaar Number Entry' on the page. Next, select 'Search Element' as Registration number or License. Then, enter your Registration or License number and click on 'Get Details' icon.

Article Title: iOS 27: Apple’s iOS 27 may add redesigned Siri app with extensions support
Article Text:
Apple's Siri

Siri Extensions: An AI App Store



Hardware still remains at the core of Apple







Apple is preparing to unveil its next major software update — iOS 27 at its upcoming Worldwide Developers Conference scheduled to take place on June 8. According to a report by Bloomberg’s Mark Gurman the company is expected to introduced a redesigned Siri app with a new “Extentions” feature with iOS 27 update. The report suggests that this move of the Cupertino-based tech giant is reflects its acknowledgement that its in-house AI technology lags behind rivals like ChatGPT, Google Gemini, and Anthropic. Rather than competing head-to-head in the AI arms race, Apple is focusing on leveraging its hardware ecosystem and App Store model.As per the Bloomberg report, the new Extensions feature will enable the users to install third-party AI chatbots apart from ChatGPT and run them directly inside Siri. Along with this, Apple is said to create a dedicated App Store section for these integrations, effectively launching an AI marketplace. This approach of the company mirrors its historical strategy: offering built-in apps while allowing third-party alternatives, and taking a cut of revenue through its 30% App Store commission.Apple is also working closely with Google’s Gemini technology in order to revamp Siri and other AI functions, making sure that its own tools remain usable out of the box.But the company recognizes that most users won’t pay for Siri alone, so third-party integrations are seen as the key to keeping iPhone users engaged while monetizing AI-driven apps.Despite its AI push, Apple’s strength remains in hardware. Its chips and devices are well-suited to running AI workloads, and future product categories — from high-end AirPods and smart glasses to smart home devices — will rely on Siri’s evolution. Analysts note this reinforces Apple’s hardware-first identity and positions John Ternus, head of hardware engineering, as a potential successor to CEO Tim Cook.Unlike competitors aggressively monetizing AI subscriptions, Apple has effectively conceded the race to build the most advanced models. Instead, it is betting that Siri Extensions will keep users loyal to iPhones while letting third parties innovate. The strategy echoes the success of the App Store but raises questions about whether Apple can afford to let rivals define the future of AI while it focuses on ecosystem control.
Article Summary: Apple is preparing to unveil its next major software update — iOS 27. The company is expected to introduced a redesigned Siri app with a new “Extentions” feature. The new Extensions feature will enable the users to install third-party AI chatbots apart from ChatGPT and run them directly inside Siri. Along with this, Apple is said to create a dedicated App Store section for these integrations, effectively launching an AI marketplace.

Article Title: Amid layoffs, Mark Zuckerberg's Meta is asking engineers to use AI for…
Article Text:
Meta has set minimum AI coding targets for engineers across divisions, with some teams expected to write over 75% of their code using AI tools like Metamate and Google Gemini. The company has also restructured parts of Reality Labs into small AI "pods" with new job titles. The AI push comes alongside layoffs that hit hundreds of employees this week across multiple divisions.

Meta wants 80% of mid-to-senior engineers using AI tools like Gemini and Metamate

Meta Reality Labs restructured into small AI 'pods' with new job titles

The push comes as Meta cuts hundreds of jobs

Meta isn't just talking about going "AI-native"—it's putting numbers on it. Internal documents reviewed by Business Insider reveal that the company has set specific targets for how much of its engineers' code should be written with AI assistance, with some teams expected to hit as high as 75%. The push comes as Meta lays off hundreds of employees and doubles down on artificial intelligence across its operations.The targets vary by division. Meta's creation org—the team behind core experiences like Messenger, WhatsApp, and Facebook—has set a first-half 2026 goal requiring 65% of its engineers to write more than 75% of their committed code using AI coding tools. The Scalable Machine Learning team, which handles AI models and infrastructure, had a February 2026 target of 50% to 80% AI-assisted code.A companywide Q4 2025 goal for central products called for 55% of software engineers' code changes to be "agent-assisted. "It's not just about code volume. The company also set a target for 80% of mid-to-senior-level engineers to adopt AI tools like DevMate, Metamate, and Google's Gemini. The focus here, according to the document, is on tool adoption rather than raw output percentages. A Meta spokesperson told Business Insider that the company's performance programme rewards impact from AI tools, not just usage.Whether these AI adoption targets are formally tied to performance reviews remains unclear. But the direction is unmistakable. Last November, Meta's head of people Janelle Gale told employees in an internal memo that "AI-driven impact" would become a core expectation starting 2026. Workers were told to highlight their AI wins in self-reviews, and Meta rolled out an "AI Performance Assistant" to help employees write their own reviews.The coding targets are one piece of a larger organisational overhaul at Meta. A separate leaked memo showed that a 1,000-person division within Reality Labs has been reorganised into small "pods," with every employee now holding one of three titles—AI Builder, AI Pod Lead, or AI Org Lead. The structure is designed to flatten hierarchies and push cross-disciplinary work, with engineers potentially taking on design tasks depending on the project.Meta's CTO Andrew Bosworth has also taken direct charge of the company's "AI for Work" initiative, which drives internal AI tool adoption company-wide.All of this is happening against a backdrop of job cuts. Meta laid off hundreds of employees this week across Reality Labs, recruitment, and other divisions. The company said the restructuring and the layoffs are unrelated—but the optics are hard to ignore. Meta is simultaneously shrinking headcount and demanding that the engineers who remain lean harder on AI to pick up the slack. CEO Mark Zuckerberg himself framed 2026 as the year AI begins to replace what once required large teams with one "very talented" person.
Article Summary: Meta has set minimum AI coding targets for engineers across divisions. Some teams expected to write over 75% of their code using AI tools like Metamate and Google Gemini. The company has also restructured parts of Reality Labs into small AI "pods" with new job titles.

Article Title: DeepSeek outage: China’s AI chatbot down for hours, longest since debut
Article Text:
DeepSeek outage



DeepSeek outage lasted several hours, users report disruptions

DeepSeek outage comes amid expectations of new update



Here’s a timeline of DeepSeek outage

China’s DeepSeek faced its biggest outage since its debut, with the AI chatbot doing down for several hours overnight. The disruption affected users across China, with many reporting issues accessing the service. According to user reports tracked on website health tracking platform Downdetector, problems began on Sunday (March 29) evening. DeepSeek later confirmed the issue on its status page, saying it detected a fault at around 9:35 p.m. The company initially marked the issue as resolved within two hours, but further updates showed continued performance problems before a fix was rolled out the next morning.DeepSeek said it implemented a fix around 9:13 a.m. on Monday and is monitoring the situation.The company did not share details about the cause of the outage. According to its official status page, all systems are now operational.The extended downtime is unusual for DeepSeek, which has maintained a high uptime record since the launch of its R1 model in January 2025. The platform has been seen as a key player in China’s AI space.The outage comes as speculation grows about a possible major update from DeepSeek. The company has not confirmed any timeline, but rivals such as Alibaba, ByteDance, and Tencent have recently launched new AI models. Expectations remain high for DeepSeek’s next move, even as it stays silent on future plans.
Article Summary: China’s DeepSeek faced its biggest outage since its debut, with the AI chatbot doing down for several hours overnight. The disruption affected users across China, with many reporting issues accessing the service. The company said it implemented a fix around 9:13 a.m. on Monday and is monitoring the situation. According to its official status page, all systems are now operational.

Article Title: Sergey Brin: Google cofounder Sergey Brin to employees at town hall: This year a big role will be played by ...
Article Text:
Google cofounder Sergey Brin’s message to employees



AI adoption becoming mandatory at Google







Google employees have been buzzing about a new internal tool called Agent Smith, named after the antagonist in The Matrix. The tool is created to automate various tasks such as coding, run asynchronously in the background, and even be accessed via mobile phones. According to a report by Business Insider, the tool became so popular within the organisation that Google had to restrict access in order to manage demand. Designed on Google’s existing agentic coding platform Antigravity, Agent Smith interacts with various internal tools and can autonomously plan and execute workflows. Unlike earlier assistants, it can pull up documents linked to employee profiles and be used directly from Google’s internal chat platform.As per the BI reports, at a recent town hall with sales employees, Google cofounder Sergey Brin also stressed on the fact that AI agents will be a major focus for Google in 2026. Brin, who returned to hands-on work at Google in 2023, said agents represent the next big leap in productivity. Along with this, he also hinted at tools similar to “OpenClaw,” though it’s unclear if he was referring to Agent Smith or another project.In the same meeting, Google’s business chief Philipp Schindler also joked that he could tell when Brin’s agent was responding to messages on his behalf underscoring how deeply these tools are already being integrated into workflows.Google’s leadership, including CEO Sundar Pichai, has been pressing employees to adopt AI tools across technical and non-technical roles. In some cases, usage of AI is now factored into performance reviews. Engineers were told last year that AI adoption was expected, and more recently, non-technical staff have been given the same directive.Beyond top-down mandates, employees themselves are experimenting with initiatives like Project EAT, aimed at standardizing AI adoption across Google’s infrastructure teams.The comments made by Brin highlight the boarder industry trend as Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is also reportedly building his own AI agent to help run the company, while other tech giants are racing to embed agentic AI into daily operations.
Article Summary: Google employees have been buzzing about a new internal tool called Agent Smith, named after the antagonist in The Matrix. The tool is created to automate various tasks such as coding, run asynchronously in the background, and even be accessed via mobile phones. According to a report by Business Insider, the tool became so popular within the organisation that Google had to restrict access in order to manage demand.

Article Title: What is Anthropic’s Claude Design, and why are Figma and Adobe stocks dropping
Article Text:
Anthropic has a design tool now. Claude Design lets anyone—founders, marketers, product managers—go from a text prompt to a finished prototype or slide deck in minutes. No Figma license, no design background needed. Powered by Claude Opus 4.7, it's live for paid subscribers. Figma lost 7.5% on the news. Adobe slid over 1%. The CPO resignation three days earlier didn't help either.

Claude Design is built for people who've never opened Figma

How Claude Design learns your brand automatically

Figma lost a board member three days before this launched—now its stock is down





Anthropic has launched Claude Design, a standalone AI workspace that turns plain-text prompts into finished prototypes, slide decks, one-pagers, and marketing assets. No design background needed. The tool is live now in research preview for Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscribers, included in existing plans at no extra cost.Claude Design is powered by Opus 4.7, Anthropic's newest vision model, capable of processing images at up to 3.75 megapixels—more than three times the resolution of previous Claude models.Users describe what they want, Claude builds a first draft, and refinement happens through chat, inline comments, direct edits, or custom sliders that Claude generates on the fly for specific elements like color, spacing, and layout. The whole thing is designed to feel iterative—less like issuing commands to software, more like bouncing ideas off a fast colleague.The tool is deliberately aimed at people who aren't designers. Founders, product managers, marketers—anyone who's had an idea they couldn't visualize fast enough.Anthropic has its own testimonials to tout, naturally. Brilliant, the education platform, claims pages that took 20-plus prompts in competing tools needed just two in Claude Design.Datadog says what used to be a week-long cycle of briefs, mockups, and review rounds now fits inside a single conversation.Finished work exports cleanly as a PDF, PPTX, standalone HTML, or goes straight to Canva for further editing. There's also a one-click handoff to Claude Code, which converts a completed design into production-ready code—keeping the entire workflow inside Anthropic's ecosystem.Claude Design's onboarding has a neat trick: Point it at your company's codebase and existing design files, and it builds a working design system from scratch—your actual colors, typography, and components—applied automatically to every project after that.Teams can maintain multiple design systems and refine them over time. A web capture tool also pulls elements directly from a live website, so prototypes resemble the real product from the very first draft rather than a generic placeholder.Figma shares fell 7.5% on the news. Adobe dropped over 1%—smaller, but a signal nonetheless. The reason isn't hard to find.Claude Design is a standalone product that generates complete, interactive prototypes from plain language—accessible to anyone who can type a sentence. That's the part that stings. Both Figma and Adobe have always assumed a trained designer is somewhere in the loop. Anthropic's tool doesn't. It goes straight to the founder, the product manager, the marketer—the people who were never Figma's customers to begin with, but whose companies were.Anthropic says Claude Design is meant to complement tools like Figma and Canva, not replace them. The Canva export and PPTX support seem to back that up. But then there's this: Anthropic's chief product officer Mike Krieger resigned from Figma's board on April 14th, three days before launch, the same day reports surfaced that Anthropic was building design capabilities that competed directly with Figma's core product.
Article Summary: Claude Design turns plain-text prompts into finished prototypes, slide decks, one-pagers, and marketing assets. Powered by Claude Opus 4.7, it's live for paid subscribers. Anthropic's chief product officer Mike Krieger resigned from Figma's board three days before launch.

Article Title: Spotify's tablet app finally feels like it was made for a tablet
Article Text:
Spotify has launched a revamped app for tablets, offering a truly native experience for larger screens. The new design features a dynamic interface that adapts to both portrait and landscape modes, introducing a "parallel browsing" feature with a collapsible sidebar. This allows users to explore recommendations or their library while music continues to play, enhancing discovery on tablets.





Spotify has rolled out a redesigned app for Android and iOS tablets, and it's the first time the experience has been built specifically for larger screens rather than just stretched out.The biggest change is how the app handles orientation. Instead of simply resizing when you flip your tablet, the interface now fully reconfigures between portrait and landscape—layouts shift, not just scale. A collapsible sidebar sits alongside playback, letting you scroll through recommendations or your library without interrupting what's playing. Spotify calls this "parallel browsing," and it's exactly what it sounds like.The "Switch to Video" toggle has also been moved somewhere more accessible, making it easier to jump into a more immersive viewing experience. The bottom navigation bar stays put—Home, Search, Your Library, Create—keeping things familiar for anyone already used to the phone app.The redesign had quietly appeared for some users earlier this year during testing, but as of April 16, it's officially out for everyone on both iPad and Android.On Android, it also works on select foldables—confirmed on the Galaxy Z Fold 7, Honor Magic V6, and Pixel 9 Pro Fold, though not on the Oppo Find N6. That's likely down to aspect ratio or screen density rather than any deliberate exclusion.Nicole Burrow, Spotify's head of design for consumer experience, put it plainly: "From TV to desktop to the car, we've been designing Spotify to feel native to each screen—and now we're bringing that same experience to tablets. Tablets give you more room to explore, so we designed it to let you browse and discover new favorites alongside what's already playing. The goal was simple: Wherever you open Spotify, it should feel unmistakably like Spotify. "
Article Summary: Spotify has rolled out a redesigned app for Android and iOS tablets. The new design features a dynamic interface that adapts to both portrait and landscape modes. A collapsible sidebar sits alongside playback, letting you scroll through recommendations or your library without interrupting what's playing.

Article Title: Microsoft VP Rajesh Jha has a big business idea for software companies on Agentic AI that should wipe out all 'Claude fears'
Article Text:
‘Treat AI agents as human users’



‘Protecting the SaaS business’







As fears grow that artificial intelligence (AI) may kill the traditional software business model, a top executive at Microsoft has floated a new idea: make the AI agents pay for their own seats – just like in the case of human workers. For decades, the “Software as a Service” (SaaS) industry has relied on “seat-based pricing”, which is a system where a company pays a monthly fee for every human employee who uses a program.With AI agents now capable of doing the work of multiple people, investors have panicked, fearing that smaller human headcounts will lead to shrinking profits for giants like Microsoft, Salesforce and Workday. However, Rajesh Jha, Microsoft’s vice president of experiences and devices, addressed these concerns at a recent conference by flipping the script. He suggested that in the near future, AI agents won't just be tools; they will be digital employees with their own identities.Under Jha’s vision, an AI agent would have its own login credentials, email inbox, security permissions and access to specific software tools – making them like any other human user – and software companies may charge them on per agent basis.“All of those embodied agents are seat opportunities,” Jha explained, as per a report by Business Insider.He envisions a world where a company might have more AI agents than human workers – and every single one of them would require a paid license.This ‘math’ behind this “Agentic AI” model may be a lifesaver for the tech industry. For example, if a company has 20 employees, it buys 20 licenses. If AI allows that company to shrink to 10 humans, software revenue would normally drop by half.However, if those 10 humans are managing 50 AI agents, and each agent requires a license, the software provider actually increases its sales from 20 seats to 60.If Jha’s vision takes hold, it could effectively wipe out “Claude fears” – a term loosely used for the anxiety that highly capable AI models like Anthropic’s Claude will make traditional software seats obsolete. The fear is: if AI makes each human worker vastly more productive, companies need fewer workers, and therefore fewer licences. Revenue shrinks.This fear came after Anthropic launched specialised plugins for use in judiciary. Jha’s argument is that this fear is based on a misreading of how AI will actually be deployed.
Article Summary: Rajesh Jha, Microsoft’s vice president of experiences and devices, suggested that in the near future, AI agents won't just be tools; they will be digital employees. He envisions a world where a company might have more AI agents than human workers – and every single one of them would require a paid license.
